goal: Verify that within a bounded event window (limit=500), every RunStarted has a matching RunCompleted and every ModelCallStarted has a matching ModelCallCompleted — no orphaned starts, no unmatched completions. NOTE: This fixture is a CANARY — it currently FAILS because there are real incomplete runs and model calls in the event log. It will start passing when the lifecycle gaps are closed. Until then, a failure here means the gap still exists, not that the fixture is broken.
tests: cargo run -- state lifecycle --limit 500 --json 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); sys.exit(0 if d.get('balanced') else 1)"
hidden_failure_mode: A change to the state recording pipeline introduces event ordering bugs (completions arriving before starts) or dropped events, causing downstream diagnostics to operate on incomplete lifecycle data without any visible error. The 'balanced' field would silently become false while the canary fixture continues failing — the regression signal is indistinguishable from the pre-existing gap until the gap is closed.
